existingProposals.evidence_summary: Pilot entity: Metacarpal Fracture Key Imaging Finding

metadata.description: Characteristic imaging finding for metacarpal fracture.
Anterior interosseous nerve branch of median nerve; motor to FPL and index FDP.
Thumb metacarpal articulating with trapezium at the CMC joint.
Ulnar-sided proximal carpal bone articulating with the lunate and TFCC.
Extensor digitorum communis tendons extending MCP and IP joints.
Middle segment of digits 2–5 between PIP and DIP.
Proper digital nerves supplying sensibility to the fingers.

Proximal interphalangeal articulation governing finger flexion cascade.
Ulnar-sided distal carpal bone with hook for flexor tendon passage.
Index metacarpal with stable CMC articulation.
Ulnar collateral ligament of the thumb MCP joint; injured in skier/gamekeeper thumb.

metadata.claim_text: Metacarpal Fracture evaluation should anchor on mechanism, focused exam, and the anatomic structures most at risk (second-metacarpal, third-metacarpal, fourth-metacarpal).
Obtain appropriate hand/wrist imaging for metacarpal fracture and explicitly comment on articular alignment and adjacent joint stability.
Missing second-metacarpal assessment in metacarpal fracture is a common board and OITE trap.
Early protected motion and therapy goals for metacarpal fracture should align with tendon, ligament, and joint stability constraints.
